content.claim: Hungary's population has declined by nearly 8% since 1990, with projections indicating a further 13% decline by 2050, but this decline is spatially uneven, with North-Eastern, South-Western, and Western regions experiencing more acute depopulation than national average.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Implementation planning lens
limitations: Projections are scenario-based and sensitive to migration and fertility assumptions not detailed in notes
Sub-regional variation within named regions (e.g., within North-East) is not specified
Educational enrollment decline may not track population decline uniformly due to cohort structure effects
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
